message.content:
hi. new project anthropicprinciple.ai at ~/Claudette/Cowork/projects/anthropicprinciple/

forked from an existing project.

Plz update this Claude MD to show a sterling example of a well organised and comprehensive CLAUDE.md project file.
add title, summary, stack, file structure, technical development approach, UX goals, SEO features, performance features, content summary, layout & style guide, css conventions, standards compliance, skills, test program + anything else you can think of.

Plz update the index.html & clock-controls.html with html / css best practices - but you don't need to look at or change the grid / clock functionality - just the basic structure of the html.
Plz update the css with any best practice improvements and remove existing brand styles for default global presentation & new pages - but don't touch the how the css for the clock works - imperative that the 'clock' functionality continues to work - code starting at: 
<!-- 84 mini-clocks pre-rendered: 6 rows × 14 cols.
         Hands start at 0° (both pointing up) until clock.js applies transforms. -->
    <div class="grid" id="grid">
Plz analyse and understand this functionality before you begin - it is all working well.
Plz check the general integrity of the existing code.
Plz do all this as if you were given the instructions currently added to CLAUDE.md at the start - this is to provide github visitors with an outstanding example of how its done.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/mat/.claude/skills/frontend-standards # Front-End Coding Standards Professional front-end code. Follow these standards. They exist for consistency, maintainability, and quality. ## Core Principles - **Separation of concerns**: HTML for structure, CSS for presentation, JS for behaviour. - **Progressive enhancement**: Build for the lowest capable device first, layer up. - **Readability over cleverness**: Code is read more than written. Optimise for the next developer. - **Mobile-first CSS**: Write base styles for the smallest screen. Add complexity upward via `min-width` or `width >=` media queries. Never use `max-width` media queries for responsive breakpoints. - **Just enough JS**: Only add JavaScript when HTML/CSS cannot achieve the goal. --- ## HTML ### Doctype and encoding Every page starts with: ```html ``` ### Semantic markup Use meaningful elements: `

content.text: Now I have a thorough understanding of the entire codebase. Let me do everything in one well-organised pass.

input.content: # anthropicprinciple.ai — CLAUDE.md > **Instruction file for Claude Code.** Single source of truth for how Claude should approach this project. Follow these instructions exactly and in preference to any defaults. --- ## Summary **anthropicprinciple.ai** is a kinetic clock art piece inspired by Humans Since 1982's "A million times" installation. 84 mini analogue clocks arranged in a 6 × 14 grid perform choreographed animations that periodically resolve into a digital time display (HH:MM). A companion page (`/clock-controls.html`) lets visitors set a timezone offset or run a countdown timer, with settings persisted in `localStorage`. The project is an example of a high-quality, zero-dependency HTML/CSS/JS site built to W3C standards with a 100/100 Google PageSpeed target. --- ## Stack | Layer | Technology | |-------|-----------| | Markup | HTML5 — semantic, W3C valid | | Presentation | CSS3 — custom properties, Grid, Flexbox; no framework | | Behaviour | Vanilla JavaScript — ES2020+, IIFE modules | | Fonts | System fonts — Verdana (headings), Arial (body) | | Hosting | GitHub Pages (branch: `main`) | | CI | GitHub Actions | | Domain | anthropicprinciple.ai (CNAME) | | Local dev | `npx live-server` or VS Code Live Server — `http://127.0.0.1:5500` | **No build step. No npm. No bundler. Pure HTML/CSS/JS.** --- ## Repository & Deployment | Item | Value | |------|-------| | GitHub | https://github.com/mhavelock/anthropicprinciple | | Live site | https://anthropicprinciple.ai/ | | Branches | `dev` → development · `main` → production (GitHub Pages) | | Git workflow | See [Git Workflow](#git-workflow) below | --- ## File Structure ``` anthropicprinciple/ ├── index.html # Main clock page — full-screen art piece ├── clock-controls.html # Settings panel — mode, timezone, countdown ├── CLAUDE.md # This file — Claude Code instructions ├── CNAME # GitHub Pages custom domain ├── README.md # Public-facing project readme ├── favicon.ico # Legacy favicon fallback ├── favicon.svg # Modern SVG favicon (animated by JS) ├── favicon-96x96.png # PNG favicon ├── apple-touch-icon.png # iOS home screen icon └── site.webmanifest # PWA manifest │ ├── assets/ │ ├── audio/ # Sound files (future) │ ├── bgs/ # Background images │ ├── components/ # Component-level assets (icons in situ, etc.) │ ├── graphics/ # Illustrations, SVG artwork │ ├── icons/ # Standalone UI icon SVGs │ └── photos/ # Photography │ ├── styles/ │ ├── clock.css # ⚠️ CLOCK ONLY — reset + grid + hands + animations │ ├── colors.css # Design tokens — colours, spacing, typography, transitions │ ├── fonts.css # Font family declarations and heading/body assignments │ ├── global.css # Base element defaults, links, buttons, forms, 12-col grid │ ├── components.css # Reusable UI components — header, logo, etc. │ ├── controls.css # Page-specific styles for clock-controls.html │ ├── utilities.css # Helper classes — u-sr-only, u-flex-center, etc. │ ├── banner.css # Banner section component │ └── border-effect.css # Animated conic-gradient border component │ ├── js/ │ ├── clock.js # ⚠️ Clock animation engine — do not modify structure │ ├── favicon-animator.js # Animated canvas favicon — runs on all pages │ ├── logger.js # Dev logger — localStorage/sessionStorage; separate from app code │ └── main.js # Application entry point (future expansion) │ └── docs/ ├── ARCHITECTURE.md # System architecture overview ├── digit-reference.md # Hand-angle reference for all 10 digits └── summaries/ # Session handoffs, status reports, change logs ``` ### ⚠️ Protected files — do not modify clock behaviour | File | Why protected | |------|--------------| | `styles/clock.css` | Contains the grid layout and hand animation tokens. Changing it breaks the clock. | | `js/clock.js` | The animation engine — hand-angle tables, patterns, timing loop. | | `js/favicon-animator.js` | Works correctly. No changes needed. | --- ## Technical Development Approach - **No dependencies.** No npm, no bundler, no framework. Everything is plain HTML/CSS/JS. - **CSS-first.** Use CSS for UI state wherever possible (`:checked`, `:focus-visible`, `@media`, custom properties, `@keyframes`). Add JS only when CSS cannot achieve the goal. - **Separation of concerns.** HTML = structure and content. CSS = all presentation. JS = behaviour only. No inline styles. No inline event handlers. - **Progressive enhancement.** The clock grid renders statically without JS (hands at 0°). JS then animates them. - **Modular JS.** Each JS file is a self-contained IIFE or module. No globals. No shared mutable state across files. - **CSS custom properties.** All colours, spacing, and timing values are defined as `--custom-properties` in `colors.css`. Never hard-code values in component CSS. - **Mobile-first CSS.** Base styles target the smallest screen. Use `min-width` media queries to layer on complexity for larger screens. Never use `max-width` for responsive breakpoints. - **`requestAnimationFrame` loop.** `clock.js` uses `rAF` for smooth 60fps animation — no `setInterval` for the render loop. - **`localStorage` persistence.** Clock settings (mode, UTC offset, countdown end time) survive page reloads. - **Flex for layout, Grid for components.** Use Flexbox for page-level flow. CSS Grid for components where a two-dimensional structure is semantically correct (e.g. the clock grid, the 12-column page grid). - **Chained classes.** Separate structural and presentational concerns using chained classes where it improves clarity. --- ## UX Goals - **Immediate delight.** The animation plays instantly on load — no spinner, no splash screen. - **Minimal chrome.** On the main page, the clock fills the full viewport. Nothing competes. - **Discoverable controls.** `/clock-controls.html` is accessible via keyboard and linked clearly. - **Settings persist automatically.** No save button needed — `localStorage` updates on every change. - **Large touch targets.** All interactive controls ≥ 44 × 44 px. Labels, inputs, and buttons are large and readable. - **Clean and minimal.** No decoration that doesn't serve the experience. - **Orientation aware.** The clock grid adapts gracefully to landscape on small-screen devices. - **Reduced motion respect.** The controls page respects `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`. - **Keyboard accessible.** Every interactive element reachable by Tab, operable by Enter/Space, dismissable by Escape. --- ## SEO Features - Descriptive `` on every page. - `<meta name="description">` on every page. - Open Graph `og:` tags — title, description, type, image, url. - `<link rel="canonical">` on every page. - Schema.org JSON-LD structured data (`WebSite`, `WebApplication`). - Semantic HTML5 landmarks: `<main>`, `<header>`, `<nav>`, `<footer>` where appropriate. - `lang="en"` on `<html>`. - `<meta name="robots" content="index, follow">` on indexable pages. - `site.webmanifest` for PWA and search discoverability. --- ## Performance Features - Zero third-party scripts or stylesheets on `index.html`. - Critical CSS (`clock.css`) loaded as a single `<link>` in `<head>`. - System fonts only — zero font-loading requests on the main clock page. - All `<img>` elements have `width`, `height`, `alt`; below-fold images use `loading="lazy"`. - `srcset` for responsive images at 1× and 2× density (retina) descriptors. - `<link rel="preconnect">` for any external domains. - Explicit image dimensions prevent Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS). - `defer` attribute on all `<script>` tags. - `requestAnimationFrame` for the clock render loop — no layout-triggering properties animated. - CSS animations use `transform` and `opacity` only. - `aspect-ratio` on the clock grid prevents CLS on load. - **Target: Google PageSpeed ≥ 95 on both mobile and desktop.** --- ## Content Summary | Page | Content | |------|---------| | `index.html` | Full-screen kinetic clock. 84 mini analogue clocks animate through 4 choreographed patterns, then resolve to display the current time (HH:MM). Cycles every 30 seconds. | | `clock-controls.html` | Settings panel. Toggle between clock mode (UTC offset ±12h) and countdown mode (MM:SS). Settings persist in `localStorage` and take effect on `index.html` immediately. | --- ## Layout & Style Guide ### Grid - **12-column grid** for page-level layouts (`.container`, `.row`, `.col-*`). - **Gutter:** `1rem` (16px at browser default). - **Max content width:** `1440px`. - **Minimum element width:** `288px` (prevents over-compression on small devices). - **Clock page:** single full-viewport Flexbox container — no page grid. ### Typography | Role | Font stack | Weight | |------|-----------|--------| | Headings h1–h6 | `Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif` | bold | | Body, paragraphs | `Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif` | normal | | Monospace / code | `'Courier New', Courier, monospace` | normal | - Base: `1rem = 16px` (browser default — do not override `font-size` on `html` or `body`). - All font sizes in `rem`. All spacing in `rem` (or `em` for component-relative values). - Responsive line height: `1.5` base; `1.7` at ≥ 768px. ### Colour Palette | Token | Light Mode | Dark Mode | |-------|-----------|-----------| | `--color-bg` | `#f5f6f7` | `#0e0f12` | | `--color-bg-alt` | `#ebeef0` | `#1a1c20` | | `--color-text` | `#1d1f24` | `#a1b1ca` | | `--color-text-muted` | `#555e64` | `#7a8896` | | `--color-btn-primary` | `#a1b1ca` | `#1d1f24` | | `--color-text-on-btn-primary` | `#1d1f24` | `#a1b1ca` | | `--color-btn-secondary` | `#3e3b1b` | `#ebe6b7` | | `--color-text-on-btn-secondary` | `#a1b1ca` | `#1d1f24` | | `--color-link` | `#1d1f24` | `#a1b1ca` | | `--color-focus` | `#1d1f24` | `#a1b1ca` | All tokens defined in `styles/colors.css`. All dark-mode overrides via `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` and `[data-theme="dark"]`. ### Spacing Scale | Token | Value | px equivalent | |-------|-------|--------------| | `--space-xs` | `0.25rem` | 4px | | `--space-sm` | `0.5rem` | 8px | | `--space-md` | `1rem` | 16px | | `--space-lg` | `1.5rem` | 24px | | `--space-xl` | `2rem` | 32px | | `--space-2xl` | `3rem` | 48px | | `--space-3xl` | `4rem` | 64px | ### Breakpoints (mobile-first, min-width only) | Name | Value | Context | |------|-------|---------| | `sm` | `480px` | Large phones | | `md` | `768px` | Tablets | | `lg` | `1024px` | Laptops | | `xl` | `1280px` | Desktops | | `2xl` | `1440px` | Wide screens | --- ## CSS Conventions - **File per concern.** One responsibility per stylesheet. Never mix concerns. - **Load order for non-clock pages:** `colors.css` → `fonts.css` → `global.css` → `components.css` → `[page].css` → `utilities.css`. - **Clock page only:** `clock.css` (self-contained — contains its own reset, tokens, grid, and animation styles). - **Component naming pattern:** - `.component` — root: `.card` - `.component-element` — child: `.card-title`, `.card-body` - `.mod-variant` — modifier: `.mod-featured` - `.is-state` — JS-applied state: `.is-open`, `.is-active` - `.js-hook` — JS selector only, never styled: `.js-toggle` - `.u-utility` — utility: `.u-sr-only`, `.u-flex-center` - **No inline styles.** Never use the `style` attribute. - **No `!important`.** Find a lower-specificity solution. - **Alphabetical properties** within each rule block. - **`:focus-visible` not `:focus`** — keyboard focus indicators only. - **Hover, focus, active states — no visited state.** - **Mobile-first always.** `@media (min-width: …)` only. Never `max-width`. - **`box-sizing: border-box`** in every stylesheet reset. - **CSS custom properties** for every colour, spacing value, and transition duration. --- ## Standards Compliance - HTML5, W3C valid. - WCAG 2.1 AA. - `lang="en"` on `<html>`. - `<meta charset="UTF-8">` first in `<head>`. - `<!doctype html>` lowercase. - Semantic landmarks: `<main>`, `<header>`, `<nav>`, `<footer>`. - Every `<input>` and `<textarea>` paired with a `<label for="…">`. - Every `<img>` has `alt`, `width`, `height`. - Keyboard: Tab → interactive elements, Enter/Space → activate, Escape → dismiss overlays. - Visible `:focus-visible` ring on all interactive elements. - WCAG AA colour contrast: 4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for UI components. - `prefers-color-scheme` for automatic dark mode. - `prefers-reduced-motion` for animation opt-out. - Schema.org JSON-LD structured data on each page. - One `<h1>` per page; heading hierarchy sequential. - `defer` on all `<script>` tags. --- ## Skills | Skill | When to invoke | |-------|---------------| | `frontend-standards` | Any time HTML, CSS, or JS is written, reviewed, or refactored | | `git-commit-messaging` | Every commit — produces Conventional Commits format messages | --- ## Test Programme After each significant change, run this review prompt: > "Taking into account the best practices docs in the project `docs/` folder, please run a code review on what we have. Look for better code organisation, clean code improvements, performance improvements, browser memory usage issues, and a security check. Update the md docs and memory file then add and commit to git. Suggest any additional best practice improvements and anything important I may have forgotten. Check changes didn't break completed task functionality." ### Mat's Checklist - [ ] Clock animation runs — patterns cycle correctly, time resolves at correct interval - [ ] Countdown mode: set timer, verify display counts down, zero state pulses red - [ ] `localStorage` persists settings across page reload and between pages - [ ] Controls page: all inputs save to `localStorage` on change without requiring Save - [ ] Keyboard: Tab through all controls on controls page, Enter/Space activates Start button - [ ] Responsive: test at 320px, 480px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px viewport widths - [ ] Landscape: clock grid fits in viewport on small-screen landscape (e.g. iPhone landscape) - [ ] Dark mode: verified at OS system preference level - [ ] PageSpeed: Lighthouse on main page — score ≥ 95 on both mobile and desktop ### Claude's Checklist - [ ] HTML validates — W3C validator (no errors, no warnings) - [ ] All `<img>` have `alt`, `width`, `height` - [ ] All `<input>` have an associated `<label for="…">` - [ ] No `!important` in CSS - [ ] No inline styles in HTML - [ ] No `max-width` media queries (must be mobile-first `min-width`) - [ ] `box-sizing: border-box` present in every stylesheet reset - [ ] CSS custom properties used for all colours and spacing values - [ ] `defer` on all `<script>` tags - [ ] No console errors or warnings in browser devtools - [ ] `prefers-color-scheme: dark` verified visually - [ ] `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` verified (animations pause/reduce) - [ ] Schema.org JSON-LD present on each page - [ ] Open Graph meta tags present on each page --- ## Common Commands ```bash # Local dev server npx live-server # Validate HTML (requires html-validate) npx html-validate index.html clock-controls.html # Show staged + unstaged diff before committing git diff # Show what would be committed git diff --cached # Stage specific files and commit git add styles/colors.css styles/global.css git commit -m "style(css): mobile-first refactor and brand colour update" ``` --- ## Git Workflow - **Branches:** `dev` for development → `main` for production (auto-deployed via GitHub Pages). - **Commit messages:** Conventional Commits — `type(scope): description` - Types: `feat` `fix` `style` `refactor` `docs` `perf` `test` `chore` - Examples: `feat(clock): add pattern 5` · `fix(controls): persist UTC offset` · `style(css): mobile-first refactor` - **Always `git diff` before committing** — review every change. - **Stop and show `git diff` after each task** — confirm with user before proceeding. - Use the `git-commit-messaging` skill for all commit messages. --- ## Logging System `js/logger.js` provides a structured development logger, kept entirely separate from application code. ```js // Log a named event with optional data payload Logger.log('section', 'message', { optional: 'data' }); // Time measurements Logger.time('hero-image'); // start a named timer Logger.timeEnd('hero-image'); // stop timer, log elapsed ms // Inspect stored logs Logger.dump(); // print all session logs to console Logger.clear(); // clear sessionStorage log store ``` - Logs stored in `sessionStorage` (cleared on tab close). - On `beforeunload`, a summary snapshot is written to `localStorage`. - Logger is passive: it never throws, never blocks execution. - Remove `<script src="js/logger.js">` from HTML before production deployment if not needed.

All dark-mode overrides via `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` and `[data-theme="dark"]`. ### Spacing Scale | Token | Value | px equivalent | |-------|-------|--------------| | `--space-xs` | `0.25rem` | 4px | | `--space-sm` | `0.5rem` | 8px | | `--space-md` | `1rem` | 16px | | `--space-lg` | `1.5rem` | 24px | | `--space-xl` | `2rem` | 32px | | `--space-2xl` | `3rem` | 48px | | `--space-3xl` | `4rem` | 64px | ### Breakpoints (mobile-first, min-width only) | Name | Value | Context | |------|-------|---------| | `sm` | `480px` | Large phones | | `md` | `768px` | Tablets | | `lg` | `1024px` | Laptops | | `xl` | `1280px` | Desktops | | `2xl` | `1440px` | Wide screens | --- ## CSS Conventions - **File per concern.** One responsibility per stylesheet. Never mix concerns. - **Load order for non-clock pages:** `colors.css` → `fonts.css` → `global.css` → `components.css` → `[page].css` → `utilities.css`. - **Clock page only:** `clock.css` (self-contained — contains its own reset, tokens, grid, and animation styles). - **Component naming pattern:** - `.component` — root: `.card` - `.component-element` — child: `.card-title`, `.card-body` - `.mod-variant` — modifier: `.mod-featured` - `.is-state` — JS-applied state: `.is-open`, `.is-active` - `.js-hook` — JS selector only, never styled: `.js-toggle` - `.u-utility` — utility: `.u-sr-only`, `.u-flex-center` - **No inline styles.** Never use the `style` attribute. - **No `!important`.** Find a lower-specificity solution. - **Alphabetical properties** within each rule block. - **`:focus-visible` not `:focus`** — keyboard focus indicators only. - **Hover, focus, active states — no visited state.** - **Mobile-first always.** `@media (min-width: …)` only. Never `max-width`. - **`box-sizing: border-box`** in every stylesheet reset. - **CSS custom properties** for every colour, spacing value, and transition duration. --- ## Standards Compliance - HTML5, W3C valid. - WCAG 2.1 AA. - `lang="en"` on `<html>`. - `<meta charset="UTF-8">` first in `<head>`. - `<!doctype html>` lowercase. - Semantic landmarks: `<main>`, `<header>`, `<nav>`, `<footer>`. - Every `<input>` and `<textarea>` paired with a `<label for="…">`. - Every `<img>` has `alt`, `width`, `height`. - Keyboard: Tab → interactive elements, Enter/Space → activate, Escape → dismiss overlays. - Visible `:focus-visible` ring on all interactive elements. - WCAG AA colour contrast: 4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for UI components. - `prefers-color-scheme` for automatic dark mode. - `prefers-reduced-motion` for animation opt-out. - Schema.org JSON-LD structured data on each page. - One `<h1>` per page; heading hierarchy sequential. - `defer` on all `<script>` tags. --- ## Skills | Skill | When to invoke | |-------|---------------| | `frontend-standards` | Any time HTML, CSS, or JS is written, reviewed, or refactored | | `git-commit-messaging` | Every commit — produces Conventional Commits format messages | --- ## Test Programme After each significant change, run this review prompt: > "Taking into account the best practices docs in the project `docs/` folder, please run a code review on what we have. Look for better code organisation, clean code improvements, performance improvements, browser memory usage issues, and a security check. Update the md docs and memory file then add and commit to git. Suggest any additional best practice improvements and anything important I may have forgotten. Check changes didn't break completed task functionality." ### Mat's Checklist - [ ] Clock animation runs — patterns cycle correctly, time resolves at correct interval - [ ] Countdown mode: set timer, verify display counts down, zero state pulses red - [ ] `localStorage` persists settings across page reload and between pages - [ ] Controls page: all inputs save to `localStorage` on change without requiring Save - [ ] Keyboard: Tab through all controls on controls page, Enter/Space activates Start button - [ ] Responsive: test at 320px, 480px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px viewport widths - [ ] Landscape: clock grid fits in viewport on small-screen landscape (e.g. iPhone landscape) - [ ] Dark mode: verified at OS system preference level - [ ] PageSpeed: Lighthouse on main page — score ≥ 95 on both mobile and desktop ### Claude's Checklist - [ ] HTML validates — W3C validator (no errors, no warnings) - [ ] All `<img>` have `alt`, `width`, `height` - [ ] All `<input>` have an associated `<label for="…">` - [ ] No `!important` in CSS - [ ] No inline styles in HTML - [ ] No `max-width` media queries (must be mobile-first `min-width`) - [ ] `box-sizing: border-box` present in every stylesheet reset - [ ] CSS custom properties used for all colours and spacing values - [ ] `defer` on all `<script>` tags - [ ] No console errors or warnings in browser devtools - [ ] `prefers-color-scheme: dark` verified visually - [ ] `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` verified (animations pause/reduce) - [ ] Schema.org JSON-LD present on each page - [ ] Open Graph meta tags present on each page --- ## Common Commands ```bash # Local dev server npx live-server # Validate HTML (requires html-validate) npx html-validate index.html clock-controls.html # Show staged + unstaged diff before committing git diff # Show what would be committed git diff --cached # Stage specific files and commit git add styles/colors.css styles/global.css git commit -m "style(css): mobile-first refactor and brand colour update" ``` --- ## Git Workflow - **Branches:** `dev` for development → `main` for production (auto-deployed via GitHub Pages). - **Commit messages:** Conventional Commits — `type(scope): description` - Types: `feat` `fix` `style` `refactor` `docs` `perf` `test` `chore` - Examples: `feat(clock): add pattern 5` · `fix(controls): persist UTC offset` · `style(css): mobile-first refactor` - **Always `git diff` before committing** — review every change. - **Stop and show `git diff` after each task** — confirm with user before proceeding. - Use the `git-commit-messaging` skill for all commit messages. --- ## Logging System `js/logger.js` provides a structured development logger, kept entirely separate from application code. ```js // Log a named event with optional data payload Logger.log('section', 'message', { optional: 'data' }); // Time measurements Logger.time('hero-image'); // start a named timer Logger.timeEnd('hero-image'); // stop timer, log elapsed ms // Inspect stored logs Logger.dump(); // print all session logs to console Logger.clear(); // clear sessionStorage log store ``` - Logs stored in `sessionStorage` (cleared on tab close). - On `beforeunload`, a summary snapshot is written to `localStorage`. - Logger is passive: it never throws, never blocks execution. - Remove `<script src="js/logger.js">` from HTML before production deployment if not needed.

+**anthropicprinciple.ai** is a kinetic clock art piece inspired by Humans Since 1982's "A million times" installation. 84 mini analogue clocks arranged in a 6 × 14 grid perform choreographed animations that periodically resolve into a digital time display (HH:MM). A companion page (`/clock-controls.html`) lets visitors set a timezone offset or run a countdown timer, with settings persisted in `localStorage`.
+The project is an example of a high-quality, zero-dependency HTML/CSS/JS site built to W3C standards with a 100/100 Google PageSpeed target.
-Elements able to collapse width in small devices - minimum width of element = 288px
+| Presentation | CSS3 — custom properties, Grid, Flexbox; no framework |
+| Behaviour | Vanilla JavaScript — ES2020+, IIFE modules |
+| Local dev | `npx live-server` or VS Code Live Server — `http://127.0.0.1:5500` |
-Cursor pointer on all buttons & button styled links.
+**No build step. No npm. No bundler. Pure HTML/CSS/JS.**
-Custom styles elements, which differ from the standard global brand styles (above) should be added on a separate css stylesheet and extend the global styles.
-Logo, favicon and other assets added to /assets

-Create a logging system where values logged and the logging functionality is kept separate from the main code (in a single js file. This will store where  logs any activities during site build. This is useful for keeping a record of change details in case of issues without having to keep it in context. Keep it generic and add a test log to show - localStorage, sessionStorage, loading time of the hero image, and loading time of scripts.

Look for better organisation of code, clean code review, performance improvements, browser memory usage issues and a security check. update the md docs and memory file then add & commit to git. Suggest any additional best practice improvements and anything important I may have forgotten. Check changes didnt break completed task functionality."
-Use flex layout for the main layout. Only use grid layouts for components where they’re more suitable than flex.
-Use scrset to display different sized imgs at different breakpoints
-Separate out styles for component functionality and presentation. Use chained classes.
-Hover, focus & active states - no visited state.
-Check images aren’t positioned so they cannot shrink to fit inside it’s container at lower viewport widths.
+├── index.html              # Main clock page — full-screen art piece
+├── clock-controls.html     # Settings panel — mode, timezone, countdown
+├── CLAUDE.md               # This file — Claude Code instructions

+│   ├── clock.css           # ⚠️ CLOCK ONLY — reset + grid + hands + animations
+│   ├── colors.css          # Design tokens — colours, spacing, typography, transitions
+│   ├── fonts.css           # Font family declarations and heading/body assignments
+│   ├── global.css          # Base element defaults, links, buttons, forms, 12-col grid
+│   ├── components.css      # Reusable UI components — header, logo, etc.
+│   ├── utilities.css       # Helper classes — u-sr-only, u-flex-center, etc.
+│   ├── clock.js            # ⚠️ Clock animation engine — do not modify structure
+│   ├── favicon-animator.js # Animated canvas favicon — runs on all pages
+│   ├── logger.js           # Dev logger — localStorage/sessionStorage; separate from app code
+    ├── digit-reference.md  # Hand-angle reference for all 10 digits
+    └── summaries/          # Session handoffs, status reports, change logs
-Add a section to Claude.md to document the following sections:
+### ⚠️ Protected files — do not modify clock behaviour
-Organise assets folder: audio, bgs, components, graphics,  icons, photos
-Organise css files: badges, components, custom, global, grid, theme, utilities
+| `styles/clock.css` | Contains the grid layout and hand animation tokens. Changing it breaks the clock. |
+| `js/clock.js` | The animation engine — hand-angle tables, patterns, timing loop. |
-- let’s stop & check between each task - can you show me a git diff when the task is finished.
\ No newline at end of file
+- **No dependencies.** No npm, no bundler, no framework. Everything is plain HTML/CSS/JS.
+- **CSS-first.** Use CSS for UI state wherever possible (`:checked`, `:focus-visible`, `@media`, custom properties, `@keyframes`). Add JS only when CSS cannot achieve the goal.
+- **Separation of concerns.** HTML = structure and content. CSS = all presentation. JS = behaviour only. No inline styles. No inline event handlers.
+- **Progressive enhancement.** The clock grid renders statically without JS (hands at 0°). JS then animates them.
+- **Modular JS.** Each JS file is a self-contained IIFE or module. No globals. No shared mutable state across files.
+- **CSS custom properties.** All colours, spacing, and timing values are defined as `--custom-properties` in `colors.css`. Never hard-code values in component CSS.
+- **Mobile-first CSS.** Base styles target the smallest screen. Use `min-width` media queries to layer on complexity for larger screens. Never use `max-width` for responsive breakpoints.
+- **`requestAnimationFrame` loop.** `clock.js` uses `rAF` for smooth 60fps animation — no `setInterval` for the render loop.
+- **`localStorage` persistence.** Clock settings (mode, UTC offset, countdown end time) survive page reloads.

+- **Flex for layout, Grid for components.** Use Flexbox for page-level flow. CSS Grid for components where a two-dimensional structure is semantically correct (e.g. the clock grid, the 12-column page grid).
+- **Chained classes.** Separate structural and presentational concerns using chained classes where it improves clarity.
+- **Immediate delight.** The animation plays instantly on load — no spinner, no splash screen.
+- **Minimal chrome.** On the main page, the clock fills the full viewport. Nothing competes.
+- **Discoverable controls.** `/clock-controls.html` is accessible via keyboard and linked clearly.
+- **Settings persist automatically.** No save button needed — `localStorage` updates on every change.
+- **Large touch targets.** All interactive controls ≥ 44 × 44 px. Labels, inputs, and buttons are large and readable.
+- **Clean and minimal.** No decoration that doesn't serve the experience.
+- **Orientation aware.** The clock grid adapts gracefully to landscape on small-screen devices.
+- **Reduced motion respect.** The controls page respects `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`.
+- **Keyboard accessible.** Every interactive element reachable by Tab, operable by Enter/Space, dismissable by Escape.
+- Open Graph `og:` tags — title, description, type, image, url.
+- `<meta name="robots" content="index, follow">` on indexable pages.
+- Zero third-party scripts or stylesheets on `index.html`.
+- Critical CSS (`clock.css`) loaded as a single `<link>` in `<head>`.
+- System fonts only — zero font-loading requests on the main clock page.
+- All `` elements have `width`, `height`, `alt`; below-fold images use `loading="lazy"`.
+- `srcset` for responsive images at 1× and 2× density (retina) descriptors.
+- Explicit image dimensions prevent Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS).
+- `requestAnimationFrame` for the clock render loop — no layout-triggering properties animated.
+- `aspect-ratio` on the clock grid prevents CLS on load.
+- **Target: Google PageSpeed ≥ 95 on both mobile and desktop.**
+| `index.html` | Full-screen kinetic clock. 84 mini analogue clocks animate through 4 choreographed patterns, then resolve to display the current time (HH:MM). Cycles every 30 seconds. |
+| `clock-controls.html` | Settings panel. Toggle between clock mode (UTC offset ±12h) and countdown mode (MM:SS). Settings persist in `localStorage` and take effect on `index.html` immediately. |
+- **Minimum element width:** `288px` (prevents over-compression on small devices).
+- **Clock page:** single full-viewport Flexbox container — no page grid.
+| Monospace / code | `'Courier New', Courier, monospace` | normal |
+- Base: `1rem = 16px` (browser default — do not override `font-size` on `html` or `body`).
+- All font sizes in `rem`. All spacing in `rem` (or `em` for component-relative values).
+All tokens defined in `styles/colors.css`. All dark-mode overrides via `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` and `[data-theme="dark"]`.
+- **File per concern.** One responsibility per stylesheet. Never mix concerns.
+- **Clock page only:** `clock.css` (self-contained — contains its own reset, tokens, grid, and animation styles).
+- **No inline styles.** Never use the `style` attribute.
+- **Hover, focus, active states — no visited state.**
+- **CSS custom properties** for every colour, spacing value, and transition duration.
+- Every `<input>` and `<textarea>` paired with a `<label for="…">`.
+- Keyboard: Tab → interactive elements, Enter/Space → activate, Escape → dismiss overlays.
+- Visible `:focus-visible` ring on all interactive elements.
+- WCAG AA colour contrast: 4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for UI components.
+- Schema.org JSON-LD structured data on each page.
+- One `<h1>` per page; heading hierarchy sequential.
+| `frontend-standards` | Any time HTML, CSS, or JS is written, reviewed, or refactored |
+| `git-commit-messaging` | Every commit — produces Conventional Commits format messages |
+After each significant change, run this review prompt:

+> "Taking into account the best practices docs in the project `docs/` folder, please run a code review on what we have. Look for better code organisation, clean code improvements, performance improvements, browser memory usage issues, and a security check. Update the md docs and memory file then add and commit to git. Suggest any additional best practice improvements and anything important I may have forgotten. Check changes didn't break completed task functionality."
+- [ ] Clock animation runs — patterns cycle correctly, time resolves at correct interval
+- [ ] Countdown mode: set timer, verify display counts down, zero state pulses red
+- [ ] `localStorage` persists settings across page reload and between pages
+- [ ] Controls page: all inputs save to `localStorage` on change without requiring Save
+- [ ] Keyboard: Tab through all controls on controls page, Enter/Space activates Start button
+- [ ] Landscape: clock grid fits in viewport on small-screen landscape (e.g. iPhone landscape)
+- [ ] Dark mode: verified at OS system preference level
+- [ ] PageSpeed: Lighthouse on main page — score ≥ 95 on both mobile and desktop
+- [ ] HTML validates — W3C validator (no errors, no warnings)
+- [ ] `box-sizing: border-box` present in every stylesheet reset
+- [ ] CSS custom properties used for all colours and spacing values
+- [ ] No console errors or warnings in browser devtools
+- [ ] Schema.org JSON-LD present on each page
+- [ ] Open Graph meta tags present on each page
+# Show staged + unstaged diff before committing
+git commit -m "style(css): mobile-first refactor and brand colour update"
+- **Branches:** `dev` for development → `main` for production (auto-deployed via GitHub Pages).
+  - Examples: `feat(clock): add pattern 5` · `fix(controls): persist UTC offset` · `style(css): mobile-first refactor`
+- **Always `git diff` before committing** — review every change.
+- **Stop and show `git diff` after each task** — confirm with user before proceeding.
+- Use the `git-commit-messaging` skill for all commit messages.
+`js/logger.js` provides a structured development logger, kept entirely separate from application code.
+// Log a named event with optional data payload
+Logger.dump();                   // print all session logs to console
+- Logs stored in `sessionStorage` (cleared on tab close).
+- On `beforeunload`, a summary snapshot is written to `localStorage`.
+- Logger is passive: it never throws, never blocks execution.
+- Remove `<script src="js/logger.js">` from HTML before production deployment if not needed.

content.text:
This is a fresh repo with no prior commits, so `git diff` has nothing to compare against — all files are untracked. Here's a summary of everything done:

---

**CLAUDE.md** — complete rewrite as a sterling example covering: summary, stack, repo & deployment, file structure (with ⚠️ protected files), technical development approach, UX goals, SEO features, performance features, content summary, layout & style guide (grid, typography, colour palette, spacing scale, breakpoints), CSS conventions, standards compliance, skills, test programme (two checklists), common commands, git workflow, and logging system docs.

**index.html** — semantic improvements (keeping clock grid untouched):
- Lowercase `<!doctype html>`
- Added `<meta name="description">`, Open Graph tags, `<link rel="canonical">`, `<meta name="theme-color">`, `<meta name="robots">`
- Schema.org JSON-LD (`WebApplication`)
- `<main aria-label="…">` wrapping the grid
- 4-space indentation

**clock-controls.html** — restructured:
- Inline `<style>` block removed → `styles/controls.css`
- Proper favicons added
- Title updated from `erudit0rum` → `anthropicprinciple.ai`
- `<span class="field-label">` → `<label for="hours">` (proper label association)
- `<main>`, `<nav aria-label>`, `aria-labelledby` on sections
- `role="status" aria-live="polite"` on status output
- CSS classes renamed: `hidden` → `is-hidden`, `active` → `is-active`
- Script updated to match new class names

**styles/controls.css** — NEW: extracted from clock-controls.html inline styles, reorganised with proper comments, `prefers-reduced-motion` support.

**styles/colors.css** — updated brand colours to match CLAUDE.md spec (Arial/Verdana, #1d1f24/#a1b1ca palette), removed legacy erudit0rum aliases, cleaned `rgb()` modern syntax.

**styles/fonts.css** — replaced Google Fonts import with system fonts (Verdana/Arial — zero external requests).

**styles/global.css** — fixed mobile-first (all `max-width` → `min-width`), improved grid column defaults, added `prefers-reduced-motion` block.

**styles/components.css** — fixed mobile-first, simplified header/logo breakpoints.

**styles/utilities.css** — expanded with `u-` prefixed utilities per naming conventions.

**styles/banner.css** — fixed `banner {}` selector → `.banner {}` (was targeting a non-existent element), fixed mobile-first, renamed `.banner-txt` → `.banner-title`.

**styles/border-effect.css** — fixed mobile-first, renamed classes to component-element pattern, added `aria-hidden` usage note, added `prefers-reduced-motion`.

**styles/critical.css** — replaced with explanatory stub (file was unused and had conflicting body/main rules).

**js/logger.js** — NEW: structured dev logger with `sessionStorage` + `localStorage`, `time()`/`timeEnd()` for performance, self-test on init (checks both storage APIs, logs navigation timing + TTFB).

---

Ready to commit when you are — shall I run the `git-commit-messaging` skill and do the commit?

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/mat/.claude/skills/git-commit-messaging

# Git Commit Messaging

Generate commit messages that conform to the [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) specification. These messages are machine-readable, human-friendly, and form the backbone of semantic versioning and automated changelogs.

---

## Message Structure

```
<type>[optional scope]: <description>

[optional body]

[optional footer(s)]
```

Every part serves a purpose. The type + description line is the minimum viable commit. Body and footers add depth when changes are complex or have downstream implications.

---

## Types

| Type | When to use |
|------|-------------|
| `feat` | New feature or capability added to the codebase |
| `fix` | Bug fix — patches broken behaviour |
| `docs` | Documentation only (README, comments, JSDoc) |
| `style` | Formatting, whitespace, semicolons — no logic change |
| `refactor` | Code restructured without adding features or fixing bugs |
| `perf` | Performance improvement |
| `test` | Adding or correcting tests |
| `build` | Build system, dependencies, package.json, webpack, etc. |
| `ci` | CI/CD configuration (GitHub Actions, CircleCI, etc.) |
| `chore` | Maintenance tasks that don't affect src or test files |
| `revert` | Reverts a previous commit |

When in doubt between two types, pick the one that best describes the *primary intent* of the change.

---

## Scope (optional)

A noun in parentheses describing which part of the codebase was changed:

```
feat(auth): add OAuth2 login flow
fix(cart): prevent negative quantity values
refactor(api): extract rate-limiting middleware
```

Use scope when it meaningfully narrows context. Omit it when the change is broad or the repo is small. Scope should match the team's existing conventions if any exist — check recent commits for patterns.

---

## Description

- Immediately follows the `: ` after type/scope
- Imperative mood, present tense: `add`, `fix`, `update` — not `added`, `fixes`, `updating`
- No capital letter at the start
- No full stop at the end
- Under 72 characters ideally; definitely under 100

Good: `fix(parser): handle empty array edge case`
Bad: `Fixed the parser to handle arrays that are empty.`

---

## Body (optional)

Include a body when the *why* or *how* isn't obvious from the description alone. Separate from the description with one blank line.

```
feat(checkout): add address validation on submit

Previously, invalid addresses could reach the payment step, causing
silent failures in the Stripe webhook. Validation now runs client-side
on form submit and server-side before charge creation.
```

The body is free-form. Write as many paragraphs as needed. Focus on motivation and context, not a line-by-line description of code (the diff does that).

---

## Footers (optional)

Footers follow the body (or description, if no body), separated by a blank line. Each footer is a token, a separator, and a value:

```
Reviewed-by: Alice <alice@example.com>
Refs: #142
BREAKING CHANGE: the `parseConfig` function now requires an options object
```

**Breaking changes** must be flagged one of two ways:

1. Footer: `BREAKING CHANGE: <description of what changed and migration path>`
2. `!` in the type/scope prefix: `feat(config)!: require options object in parseConfig`

Both are valid. You can use both together. If using `!`, you may omit the footer, but the footer gives consumers more migration detail.

---

## Worked examples

**Simple bugfix:**
```
fix(nav): correct active link highlight on mobile
```

**New feature with scope:**
```
feat(search): add debounced autocomplete to product search
```

**Refactor with body:**
```
refactor(db): replace raw queries with query builder

Direct SQL strings were scattered across three service files, making
schema changes risky. Centralised through Knex query builder to
improve testability and allow easier schema migrations.
```

**Breaking change with footer:**
```
feat(api)!: remove v1 endpoints

BREAKING CHANGE: /api/v1/* routes have been removed. Migrate to /api/v2/*.
See migration guide at docs/v2-migration.md.
```

**Dependency update:**
```
build(deps): upgrade Tailwind to v4

Refs: #201
```

**Multi-footer commit:**
```
fix(auth): prevent session hijack on logout

Clear all active session tokens on logout rather than just the
current one, to invalidate concurrent sessions.

Reviewed-by: Mat Havelock <mat@example.com>
Fixes: #87
```

---

## Working from staged changes or a diff

When the user asks you to generate a commit message from their current changes:

1. Run `git diff --staged` (or `git diff HEAD` if nothing is staged) to read the actual diff
2. Identify the primary intent — what is fundamentally changing?
3. Identify the scope — which module, component, or file area is the focus?
4. Check for breaking changes — are any public APIs, interfaces, or contracts altered?
5. Draft the message and show it to the user before running `git commit`

If the diff spans multiple unrelated concerns, flag this. A commit should ideally represent one logical change. You can suggest splitting if appropriate, but don't be pedantic about it — real projects often have mixed commits.

---

## Working from a description

When the user describes what they did in plain English:

- Extract the type from what was accomplished
- Extract the scope from what area was affected
- Rewrite the description in imperative form
- Ask if there's a body-worthy reason for the change (only if it's non-obvious)
- Propose the message and confirm before committing

---

## Checking existing conventions

Before generating a message, if the repo has a commit history, it's worth a quick glance:

```bash
git log --oneline -10
```

Match existing scope naming conventions if they're consistent. If the project already uses `feat(ui):` don't introduce `feat(frontend):` without reason.

---

## Output format

Always present the commit message in a code block so it's easy to copy:

```
feat(auth): add JWT refresh token rotation

Refresh tokens are now single-use and rotated on each request.
Previous tokens are invalidated immediately, reducing the window
for token theft.

BREAKING CHANGE: clients must handle 401 responses and re-authenticate
when a refresh token is rejected.
```

Then offer to run the commit directly if the user wants, using:

```bash
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
<message here>
EOF
)"
```

Use the heredoc form to preserve multiline messages correctly.

(max-width media queries replaced with min-width throughout). banner.css
selector fixed (bare `banner` element -> `.banner` class). border-effect.css
gains prefers-reduced-motion support. utilities.css expanded with u-
prefix classes.

New: styles/controls.css (extracted from clock-controls.html inline
styles), js/logger.js (sessionStorage log store, localStorage
persistence on unload, time/timeEnd performance helpers, self-test).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text: 103 cards, 103 images in HTML, 103 files on disk — perfect match. Now commit:

input.command:
git add play.html styles/play.css assets/soundcloud/ && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(play): add playlist grid page with 103 SoundCloud thumbnails

play.html: 103 playlist cards in a 2-column mobile / 6-column desktop
CSS Grid. Black background. Thumbnail images downloaded locally to
assets/soundcloud/. Title overlay positioned top-left (10px padding)
with gradient scrim for legibility on light artwork.

Link colours: rgb(153,153,153) default, white on hover/focus/active,
#4c505a visited. No underline. loading="lazy" on below-fold images;
loading="eager" on first 6. Reduced-motion respected.

styles/play.css: self-contained page styles — fixed dark theme,
aspect-ratio 1:1 cards, overflow:hidden image zoom on hover.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text: The `.gitignore` is blocking the assets folder. That's a leftover from the fork — for this project the images should be tracked. Let me fix it:

content.content:
1→.claude
     2→dist/
     3→logs/
     4→.env
     5→coverage/
     6→node_modules/
     7→/assets
     8→.vscode/

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

Primary Request and Intent: - Rewrite CLAUDE.md for anthropicprinciple.ai as a sterling, comprehensive example covering: title, summary, stack, file structure, technical development approach, UX goals, SEO features, performance features, content summary, layout & style guide, CSS conventions, standards compliance, skills, test programme, git workflow, logging system. - Update index.html and clock-controls.html with HTML/CSS best practices — but explicitly DO NOT touch the clock grid/hand functionality (`.mc`, `.hand`, `#grid`, `clock.js`, `clock.css`). - Update CSS with best practice improvements; remove old erudit0rum brand styles; replace with the project's own brand (Arial/Verdana, #1d1f24/#a1b1ca palette). Do not touch clock.css. - Create play.html: SoundCloud playlist grid scraped from /docs/discovery/soundcloud.html — download thumbnails locally, 2-col mobile / 6-col desktop, black bg, rgb(153,153,153) links (white on hover/focus/active, #4c505a visited), title overlay top-left 10px padding, max-width 1240px, min-width 320px, 20px horizontal padding, 60px top, 40px bottom. - Add 0.33rem gap between grid items on play.html. - Ensure `em` is used everywhere for typography and `rem` for distances across all CSS files. - Globally, links should be the same colour as text (light & dark mode). 2. Key Technical Concepts: - Mobile-first CSS: base styles for smallest screen, `min-width` media queries only — never `max-width` for breakpoints. - CSS custom properties for all colour, spacing, and transition tokens (colors.css as single source of truth). - Separation of concerns: HTML = structure, CSS = presentation, JS = behaviour only. No inline styles. - `em` for typography (font-size, letter-spacing), `rem` for distances (margin, padding, gap, border-radius, width, height). - Component-element CSS naming: `.card`, `.card-title`, modifiers `.mod-*`, state `.is-*`, JS hooks `.js-*`, utilities `.u-*`. - `clock.css` is self-contained and protected — contains its own reset, tokens, grid and animation styles. - `prefers-color-scheme` for automatic dark mode; `[data-theme]` for manual override. - `prefers-reduced-motion` for animation opt-out. - `loading="lazy"` on below-fold images; `loading="eager"` on first visible row. - `defer` on all `

Fonts in em and dimentions in rem. Use clean css for modern browsers and use variables for things like colours. all the global styles needed for great ux, well organised standard practices for typography, forms, buttons, hover / active states, tooltips, modal windows, alerts, svg icons. A advanced and modern approach to mobile first responsiveness. Accessibility needs and keyboard access should be catered for. keep presentation, functionality and content as separate as possible. Ensure all features needed for mobile device usage such as orientation settings. UX should be fun and easy but clean and minimal - keep text, buttons and form fields large and color scheme simple. For use with Github.

Browser default text size = 16px
12 grid layout with 1rem gutter
Elements able to collapse width in small devices - minimum width of element = 288px
Max width = 1440px

Titles: Verdana

DM text: #a1b1ca

DM Secondary Button text: #1d1f24

Cursor pointer on all buttons & button styled links.

content.text:
Here's what the audit found:

**✅ Met**
- `em` for typography, `rem` for distances
- CSS custom properties for all colours
- Mobile-first (min-width only), responsive line-height
- Arial/Verdana, brand palette LM/DM
- `cursor: pointer` on buttons, labels, checkboxes
- Min 288px / max 1440px, 12-col grid with 1rem gutter
- Flex for main layout, grid for components
- Separation of concerns (HTML/CSS/JS)
- Accessibility: `aria-*`, `focus-visible`, keyboard access
- JSON-LD schema on index.html
- Custom page styles on separate sheets (controls.css, play.css)
- Images `max-width: 100%` (can shrink to fit)
- Documentation to `/docs`

---

**⚠️ Gaps**

| Requirement | Status | Detail |
|---|---|---|
| No visited state | ❌ | `play.css` has `:visited` rules — spec says hover/focus/active only |
| `srcset` for responsive images | ❌ | No `srcset` on any images; play.html serves 500px images at all sizes |
| Tooltips, modals, alerts, SVG icons in global styles | ❌ | Not defined anywhere yet |
| Orientation settings | ❌ | No `@media (orientation: landscape)` rules |
| `/context` folder | ❌ | Spec says summaries go in `/context/summaries` — currently in `/docs/summaries` |
| Assets folder structure | ⚠️ | Spec wants: `audio/`, `bgs/`, `components/`, `graphics/`, `icons/`, `photos/` — only `icons/` and `soundcloud/` exist |
| CSS file naming | ⚠️ | Spec suggests: badges, custom, grid, theme — current naming is functionally equivalent but different |

---

The two I'd action soonest are the **`:visited` removal** (quick) and **`srcset`** (meaningful performance impact). The tooltips/modals/alerts are future-work until the pages actually need them. Want me to tackle any of these?
I'll tackle them in order of impact. Let me check components.css first, then do everything in parallel where possible:

input.command:
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import re

with open('/Users/mat/Claudette/Cowork/projects/anthropicprinciple/play.html', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

def replace_img(m):
    line = m.group(0)
    # Get the indentation (leading whitespace before <img)
    indent_match = re.match(r'^(\s*)', line)
    indent = indent_match.group(1) if indent_match else ''
    
    # Extract slug from src="assets/soundcloud/SLUG.jpg"
    slug_match = re.search(r'src="assets/soundcloud/([^"]+)\.jpg"', line)
    if not slug_match:
        return line
    slug = slug_match.group(1)
    
    # Check if it already has srcset (don't double-add)
    if 'srcset=' in line:
        return line
    
    # Build replacement: insert srcset and sizes after the src attribute
    srcset = f'srcset="assets/soundcloud/250/{slug}.jpg 250w, assets/soundcloud/{slug}.jpg 500w"'
    sizes = 'sizes="(min-width: 768px) calc((1240px - 40px) / 6), calc((100vw - 40px) / 2)"'
    
    # Replace src="..." with src="..."\n{indent}srcset="..."\n{indent}sizes="..."
    new_line = re.sub(
        r'(src="assets/soundcloud/[^"]+\.jpg")',
        f'\\1\n{indent}                        {srcset}\n{indent}                        {sizes}',
        line
    )
    return new_line

# Process line by line to handle indentation correctly
lines = content.split('\n')
result_lines = []

for line in lines:
    if 'class="playlist-card-img"' in line and 'srcset=' not in line:
        # Get leading whitespace
        indent_match = re.match(r'^(\s*)', line)
        indent = indent_match.group(1) if indent_match else ''
        
        # Extract slug
        slug_match = re.search(r'src="assets/soundcloud/([^"]+)\.jpg"', line)
        if slug_match:
            slug = slug_match.group(1)
            srcset = f'srcset="assets/soundcloud/250/{slug}.jpg 250w, assets/soundcloud/{slug}.jpg 500w"'
            sizes = 'sizes="(min-width: 768px) calc((1240px - 40px) / 6), calc((100vw - 40px) / 2)"'
            
            # Replace src="..." with src="..."\n{indent}srcset\n{indent}sizes
            new_line = re.sub(
                r'(src="assets/soundcloud/[^"]+\.jpg")',
                f'\\1\n{indent}{srcset}\n{indent}{sizes}',
                line
            )
            result_lines.append(new_line)
        else:
            result_lines.append(line)
    else:
        result_lines.append(line)

result = '\n'.join(result_lines)

with open('/Users/mat/Claudette/Cowork/projects/anthropicprinciple/play.html', 'w') as f:
    f.write(result)

print("Done")
PYEOF

content.text:
I can see three clear performance problems. Let me break them down before touching anything:

**favicon-animator.js** — worst offender:
- `setInterval` at 60ms runs independently of rAF, never pauses when tab is hidden or DevTools is open
- `canvas.toDataURL()` every 60ms forces a synchronous GPU→CPU canvas readback — expensive
- `favicon.href` DOM write 16× per second

**clock.js**:
- `requestAnimationFrame(tick)` has no visibility check — keeps running at 60fps even when tab is backgrounded or DevTools has focus
- During the 23s pattern phase, trig-heavy calculations (`Math.atan2`, `Math.sin`, `Math.sqrt`) run for all 84 cells at 60fps — could be throttled to 30fps with no visible difference

**logger.js**:
- Every single `Logger.log()` call does: `sessionStorage.getItem` → `JSON.parse` → `push` → `JSON.stringify` → `sessionStorage.setItem` — synchronous storage I/O on every log entry

---

**Plan**: pause on hidden, rAF for favicon, 30fps throttle for patterns, buffer logger writes.

input.command:
git add js/clock.js js/favicon-animator.js js/logger.js && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
perf: reduce JS CPU/memory overhead on homepage

favicon-animator.js:
- Replace setInterval with requestAnimationFrame + time-based throttle
  (~10 fps vs continuous) — rAF pauses automatically when tab is hidden
- Add visibilitychange listener to explicitly cancel/resume rAF
- canvas.toDataURL() now called ~10× per second instead of ~16×

clock.js:
- Throttle the 23 s pattern phase to ~30 fps (was 60 fps)
  — trig-heavy per-cell calculations (atan2, sqrt, sin) halved
- Store rafId and add visibilitychange listener to cancel rAF when tab
  is hidden, resume on return — eliminates background CPU usage
- Ease-in/out windows (2 s each) retain 60 fps for visual smoothness

logger.js:
- Buffer all log entries in memory (_buf array); remove synchronous
  sessionStorage read/parse/write on every log() call
- Flush buffer to sessionStorage only on beforeunload (+ manual flush())
- Consolidate navEntry lookups into single object; remove duplicate
  performance.getEntriesByType call

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
